what_it_does: A preview card (HTML) demonstrating the icon component's tone and size parameters: it renders the same five icons at five sizes (16/20/24/32/40 px) across four tone rows (bronze, gold, ink, and cream on a dark background), each tone mapped to a CSS-variable colour, pulled from the shared cv-icons.js data object.
observations: Carries a @dsCard annotation (group='Brand', name='Icon — tones & sizes', subtitle 'Bronze · gold · ink · muted · cream', viewport='700x280').
Defines TONES mapping bronze→var(--accent-bronze), gold→var(--accent-gold), ink→var(--fg-primary), cream→var(--bg-canvas).
SIZES = [16,20,24,32,40] and ICONS = ['house','file','chat','dashboard','network']; each icon rendered at the size with matching index.
The 'cream' tone is shown on a .stage.dark with var(--bg-dark) background so the light glyph is visible.
States defaults in the meta row: tone='bronze', size={24}, stroke fixed at 1.5px.
Loads ../assets/icons/cv-icons.js and reads window.CV_ICONS.data[name]; skips a stage if its element is missing.
standouts: Tones are expressed as references to design tokens (CSS vars), not literal colours — the icon's colour vocabulary is single-sourced.
Subtitle mentions five tones (bronze/gold/ink/muted/cream) but the rows render only four (bronze/gold/ink/cream) — 'muted' is named but not shown.
conventions: Maps a component prop (tone) to design tokens rather than raw values, consistent with the reference-not-copy directive.
concerns: Subtitle lists 'muted' as a tone but no muted row is rendered — a mismatch between the card's stated tones and the demonstrated set.
Inline style literals (font-family/size/color) repeated in the lead's <code> elements.
notes: A brand card showing the icon component's tone (token-mapped) and size axes across four example rows; note the subtitle lists 'muted' which the body does not render.
questions: Is 'muted' an intended tone that was dropped from the rendered rows, or a stale subtitle?
Is the size-per-icon pairing illustrative only, or does it reflect recommended sizes for those specific icons?
purpose_vs_actual: Mostly matches — it demonstrates tones and sizes as the @dsCard claims, but the subtitle promises a 'muted' tone that the rendered rows omit.
apparent_themes: Token-mapped component props (tone = a reference to a design token)
Parametric component axes (tone, size) shown by example
intention_signals: Aims to show that the icon component is parametric — its colour comes from named tones bound to tokens, and its size is a free axis — proving icons inherit the system's palette rather than carrying their own colours.
novelty: Demonstrates a component's colour API as a small enum of token-bound tones (including an on-dark case), making the icon's colour single-sourced from the palette.
contribution: Contributes the demonstration of the icon component's tone/size parameter surface, mapping tone names to design tokens and showing the on-dark rendering case.
summary_for_embedding: An HTML preview card rendering five icons at five sizes across four token-bound tone rows (bronze/gold/ink/cream-on-dark) from the ConceptV shared icon data object.
intention_for_embedding: To show the icon component's parametric tone and size axes, with tones bound to design tokens rather than literal colours.
